purpose: Document shape-fit gaps and AST-detection partial-coverage cases per validator mandate razor_java_constructor_carry_forward_documented. Mirrors field_access_info-extension-v1's Ruby \bgets\b carry-forward pattern.

carry_forwards.wiring: AstSinkPattern entry uses raw-fallback ("", "@Html.Raw(") in CSHARP_AST_SINKS
carry_forwards.rationale: Razor's `@` operator-prefix is a template-syntax marker, not a member-access or call_expression in tree-sitter-c-sharp's grammar (which targets pure C# — not embedded cshtml). The structural path (extract_member_access_receiver_and_field) cannot decompose `@Html.Raw(` into (receiver=Html, field=Raw) when the `@` is part of the descendant text. The raw-fallback substring path catches it via descendant_text.contains("@Html.Raw(").
carry_forwards.limitations: Only fires when the descendant whose text contains `@Html.Raw(` is in code context (filtered by is_in_string / is_in_comment upstream). Embedded cshtml files often have a tree-sitter HTML host grammar with C# islands; this M2 wiring assumes pure-C#-context Razor usage in code blocks (`.cs` files using Razor helpers via @using directives or `.cshtml` files where the tree-sitter-c-sharp parser is selected for analysis). Coverage parity with vuln.rs L407 substring matching is preserved.
carry_forwards.future_work: If a future grammar refinement (e.g., tree-sitter-cshtml) separates the `@` token from the call expression, the (Html, Raw) structural entry already in CSHARP_AST_SINKS handles the bare call shape; the raw-fallback then becomes redundant but harmless.

carry_forwards.wiring: AstSinkPattern entry uses raw-fallback ("", "echo "), ("", "print "), ("", "<?= ") in PHP_AST_SINKS
carry_forwards.rationale: PHP's `echo` and `print` are language statement-keywords (echo_statement / print_intrinsic node kinds), not call_expression. The short-tag `<?= ` is a tag_open token. None of these are call_kinds so the call-shape path at member_patterns_match L3574-L3594 and the call_names path at detect_sinks_ast L3914-L3922 do not match.
carry_forwards.ast_match_status: AST descendant matching DOES fire correctly via the raw-fallback substring path (L3601-L3605) — descendant_text.contains("echo ") matches the echo_statement node text.
carry_forwards.var_extraction_limitation: However, sink emission at L3978-L3987 requires a successful `var` extraction (otherwise `if let Some(var) = var` short-circuits). The five extraction paths (extract_call_arg, extract_sink_var_from_statement, extract_first_identifier_arg_ast, extract_assignment_rhs_ident, call-name-leading-identifier-fallback) all expect call-shape descendants. PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic / tag_open lack the structural arguments list, so var extraction silently returns None and the AST hit is dropped.
carry_forwards.current_behavior: PHP echo $name; with $name tainted from $_GET produces ZERO sink_type=html_output findings under tldr taint at M2. The AstSinkPattern is correctly entered (verifiable via cargo test sink-bank inspection); the limitation is in the var-extraction infrastructure shared across all language sinks.
carry_forwards.future_work: M3 (canonical compute_taint_with_tree dispatch via vuln.rs collapse) is the natural place to add a PHP-specific `extract_echo_print_var_php` helper that walks the echo_statement / print_intrinsic descendants for the first variable_name child. This is a sibling task to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast at L3622. Alternatively, a M5 carry-forward style fix can wire it into extract_assignment_rhs_ident's family.
carry_forwards.vuln_rs_pre_m2_behavior: vuln.rs's pre-M3 substring scanner (linear two-pass at L887-L933) DOES catch PHP echo via simple line.contains("echo ") + line.contains(tainted_var). Per validator mandate, the M1 RED tests for php_xss positive will transition to GREEN at M3 when vuln.rs collapses onto canonical AND the PHP var-extraction extension lands.
carry_forwards.blocking_for_m3: soft — requires PHP echo var-extraction extension or accepting that vuln.rs-projection-to-canonical loses PHP echo coverage (NOT acceptable per stop-thresholds; address at M3).
